programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death after stepping out of her shower, becoming the latest victim of the Dead Rose Slasher. Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: The Day of the Welsh Ferret

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Ellen Mallam is left to drown in an upturned car by its driver, but the young woman's connection to James Blackstone, an aristocratic family's heir apparent, impedes Gently and Bacchus's investigation. The chief inspector discovers that the son's future has been carefully mapped out since birth by his implacable mother Alethea, and she will not countenance any interference in her plans. However, an astonishing twist causes the detectives to reconsider the case in its entirety. Sixties-set detective drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Geraldine Somerville, James Norton, Roger Lloyd Pack, Christopher Fairbank and Ebony Buckle.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness

programme.desc: An investigation gets under way when barrister Luke Watchman dies of cyanide poisoning during his annual holiday at an inn. Crime drama, starring Patrick Malahide, Kevin McNally and William Simons.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back in 1983, 16-year-old Amy Taskerland was murdered on the night of the school disco. More than 30 years later, the unearthing of a time capsule and the discovery of her voice on a mix tape inside prompts Miller and co to delve back into the case, starting by interviewing Amy's best friend Harriet and her former teacher Mr Hines. The recording features a strange mix of music and poetry, including words from a speech written in secret for the Queen for broadcast in the event of a nuclear war. But how did Amy get a copy of it, and was its secret nature the reason she was killed? Elsewhere, Miller enjoys her first romance since her divorce, while Gerry goes on his future son-in-law's stag night. Amy Nuttall (Emmerdale, Downton Abbey) and Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe) guest star.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Emilia Fox joins the cast of the crime drama as Dr Nikki Alexander, who uses the team's equipment to solve an Iron Age mystery, but ends up bringing her expertise to bear on a modern murder. The case in question is the death of a jockey crushed by his mount during a race. The plot thickens when two of the horses' owners are killed in a helicopter crash and evidence emerges that both the horse and the pilot were drugged. James Wilby and Sylvestra Le Touzel guest star.(n)

programme.desc: Comedy about two sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who have no idea the other exists until their father dies. Cat wants to spend as much time as possible with Cathy to make up for the last 28 years and, as they were born just five days apart, decide to organise a joint birthday party. In the wake of their dad's demise, the pair struggle to find his will, while their mothers war over which family the late two-timer loved most. Stephen Tompkinson co-stars, alongside Ellie White, Lauren Socha and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: It's John's grandmother's birthday, but he has completely forgotten to post her card. Will he be able to put things right on the journey to work? Comedy about the daily commute of supermarket employees John Redmond and Kayleigh Kitson, who have been thrown together in a company car share scheme, with each trip bringing fresh insight into their lives, as well as twists and turns in their unlikely relationship. Co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson, with Danny Swarsbrick.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a couple's home in Margate. The property was originally a B&B, and the transition to a home has not been entirely successful. It is poorly laid out, has too many rooms, and while big on floor space is lacking natural light. Laura sets out to return the building to its early 20th-century roots, whole Robert comes up with a radically different way of using the living space.(n)

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part two of two. The historian and his team continue down the Colorado River in antique wooden boats as they re-create the pioneering 280-mile expedition through the Grand Canyon that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Along the way they carry out the same surveying tasks as the original party with period equipment, including taking photographs with a 19th-century 3D camera.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen embark on the third leg of the tour around Britain, following Samuel Chamberlain's 1963 travelogue British Bouquet: An Epicurean Tour of Britain. Arriving in York, they sample one of the city's signature hams, and the confusingly named savoury duck, before heading to Ampleforth for more culinary treats. Finally, their visit concludes with a riotous Viking feast, hosted by Eric the Red.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure

programme.title: Flog It!

programme.title: Animal Park

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.sub-title: Back in Time for the Weekend
programme.desc: The Ashby-Hawkins family strut into the 1970s and some discover there were unexpected upsides to the economic and political turmoil the decade is often remembered for. Rob and Steph share more time together, playing darts with Eric Bristow and taking on some period appropriate home improvement. Meanwhile, daughter Daisy lets her hair down at a roller disco with DJ Trevor Nelson, and the family receive a visit from Top Gear's original presenter, Angela Rippon.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch

programme.title: Cardinal
programme.desc: When a new victim is found, it becomes increasingly clear that the murderer's main targets are not the victims themselves, but their loved ones. Police work to establish a link between the two deaths, but crucial information continues to elude them. Meanwhile, John and Liz grow closer as they focus on the case. Crime thriller, based on the John Cardinal Mysteries series by Canadian author Giles Blunt, starring Billy Campbell, Karine Vanasse, Glen Gould and Kristen Thomson.(n)

programme.title: Cardinal
programme.desc: Another abduction hits painfully close to home for Lise, as she and John race against time to save the next victim before it is too late. programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton visits some of the most beautiful and remote spots in Britain, meeting the people who live there and discovering more about Scotland's best-kept secrets. In this episode, he visits the low-lying islands that face the full might of the Atlantic Ocean.(n)

programme.title: House of Saud: A Family at War
programme.desc: How some of the most prominent and richest Saudi royals were caught up in an unprecedented crackdown on corruption. Hundreds of the richest people in the Kingdom, including 11 members of the Royal Family, were kept in a luxurious five-star hotel. The detentions opened the door on a highly secret world of kickbacks and bribes on a monumental scale.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The friends go all out to make Susie's birthday a night to remember, and as the celebration begin, Britt is keen to show she still has what it takes by showing off her singing and dancing talents. The senior citizens also head to Chennai, where Zandra is reunited with a friend from 40 years ago and Henry relives some difficult memories of his last overseas broadcast at the city's famous cricket ground. Back in Puducherry, John and Duncan test their fitness by jumping on their bikes, while Paul, revelling in the chaos of the Indian roads, decides to take his mock Indian driving test.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A skier suffers a leg injury in a remote part of the Alps, so her guide tries to carry her to safety on his back. A girl calls 999 when her mum goes into anaphylactic shock.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: A new format of the annual wildlife series in keeping with Covid-19 lockdown conditions, which sees the presenters broadcasting live from locations around the UK. Chris Packham is in the New Forest, Iolo William is in the heart of west Wales, and Gillian Burke is at the Beaver project near her home in Cornwall. In this first episode, they are joined by guest presenter Steve Backshall at his home on the banks of the Thames. Plus, Michaela Strachan calls live from South Africa to present some of her favourite moments from past series.(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore the Co Down coastline between Belfast and Strangford Lough, along the Ards Peninsula. In Belfast, Adrian road-tests the largest hydraulic crane in the world and helps park a 100-metre-long Dutch cargo ship. Mehreen goes kayaking on Strangford Lough to see how the currents of the UK's largest sea inlet is placing the area at the forefront of renewable energy research. Plus, Helen Skelton tests the waters of the North Channel, one of the most dangerous swims in the world, and local comedian Shane Todd visits a long-established bespoke racing-car factory.(n)

programme.title: Atlantic: The Wildest Ocean on Earth
programme.desc: Cillian Murphy narrates a documentary examining the lives of animals and people pitting themselves against the world's wildest ocean, unveiling the natural forces that underpin life in the Atlantic, from the world's most powerful currents, ferocious hurricanes and huge chains of undersea volcanoes. The first episode looks at the influence of the Gulf Stream, with whales, giant turtles and jellyfish riding the huge current that becomes a migration super-highway and helps warm northern Europe, while fishermen battle for survival in mountainous seas as they try to exploit its natural fertility.(n)

programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey around the nation, with a visit to Evesham in Worcestershire, where they sample a pork pie wedding cake, meet a plum charmer, dine in one of Britain's oldest inns, and attend an asparagus festival.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Netherlands v Czech Republic. A chance to see the Euro 2004 Group D encounter at Estadio Municipal de Aveiro in Portugal.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len Goodman and Ainsley Harriott reach Cardiff, where the Strictly judge has his first taste of spaghetti and samples some German hospitality, and Ainsley cooks an Italian dish.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire as hundreds of visitors arrive with their family heirlooms and the experts pick out the best items to go off to auction. Paul also investigates the history of nearby Quarrybank Mill.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A mystery illness has the keepers worried about Kaiser the giraffe, and the whole team is on tenterhooks as the future of the entire herd hangs in the balance.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.sub-title: Back in Time for the Weekend
programme.desc: The Ashby-Hawkins family embrace the 1980s, where TV and shopping dominated the UK's leisure time. It's also the decade where technology arrives in homes in a significant way. Kids Daisy and Seth are thrilled by the new VCR and home computer, and there's even a pager for mum Steph who's now a shoulder padded businesswoman. When dad Rob is not trying out the kids' CB radio, he's going shopping for a Don Johnson makeover with fashion guru Caryn Franklin.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: On the storm-lashed Cornish coastline, a pair of shags struggle to rear their chicks, while a young female pine marten prepares to rear her young on a remote Scottish peninsular. Plus, there's a moment of magic as the team celebrates the epic beauty of Dartmoor. Presented by Chris Packham, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the high-brow quiz, asking questions about road, rail and other things beginning with the letter R. Trying to come up with the answers are Alan Davies, Aisling Bea, Cally Beaton and Holly Walsh.(n)

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: The team is given a presentation on the rules of sexual conduct and consent, after which Mattie, Benji and Jack head out for the afternoon determined to keep out of trouble. However, a chance supermarket meeting with a woman who wants them to meet her son Stuart leads to an unfortunate misunderstanding. As Stuart threatens to go public with his side of the story, Mattie and the others try to placate him with an all-access visit to the training complex to meet the team. Starring Shaquille Ali-Yebuah, Jack McMullen, Jake Short and Theo Barklem Biggs.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: Nadja and Laszlo's new familiar poses problems for an increasingly anxious Guillermo, and the vampires enlist the services of a local necromancer. Return of the comedy, starring Matt Berry, Kayvan Novak and Natasia Demetriou.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The vampires hold a seance to deal with a ghost infestation, which only leads to a larger ghost infestation. Starring Natasia Demetriou and Kayvan Novak.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: House of Saud: A Family at War
programme.desc: Examining how Saudi Arabia's most revered family stays in power, featuring interviews with those who have worked for Saudi princes. They describe how the wealth allowed lives of privilege and pleasure out of the spotlight. But the growth of social media has meant Saudis are getting increasing insight into the behaviour of their rulers, including their transgressions.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a compilation of some of the most memorable moments from the past 17 series of the business ideas contest, featuring Dragons past and present getting to grips with ingenious inventions and bizarre money-making schemes. The show recalls some of the harshest dismissals, but also the times when entrepreneurs got the chance to make their dream a reality.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga traces the history of an 18th-century sea captain's house near Bristol's docks with links to the slave trade. Bristol was a major port in the transportation of slaves from Africa's Guinea Coast to the Caribbean, and both the man who built the house and its first full-time resident were heavily involved in the trade. David uncovers stories of piracy, abandoned babies, a household slave who made a daring escape from the property, and a protest on the doorstep as the abolitionist movement gained momentum.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay shares creative and money-saving tips and tricks for the home and garden, including how to build a floating desk and upholster an old stool. Plus, Sarah Moore demonstrates a way to recycle old jars, and Monty Don shares a fantastic method of making a raised bed for the garden.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Saving Our Nurses
programme.desc: A nurse who recently arrived in the UK from the Philippines and is facing up to a double challenge - making a career in the NHS and a new life 7,000 miles from home.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an unusual musical instrument, a wartime phone being sold for charity, and a cake stand with cakes thrown in. Presented by Nigel Havers.(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores British Columbia, discovering how two superpowers nearly came to war over a pig and joining the Royal Canadian Navy to firefight on board the frigate HMCS Regina. He also explores the rich British heritage and colonial past of Victoria - the provincial capital of British Columbia - discovers the origins of the Hudson's Bay Company, helps to carve a 36- foot totem pole and learns how to head off a charging bear.(n)

programme.title: Atlantic: The Wildest Ocean on Earth
programme.desc: Documentary examining the animals pitting themselves against the world's wildest ocean, unveiling the natural forces that underpin life in the Atlantic. In the vast South Atlantic, huge pods of dolphins, massive penguin colonies and the largest gathering of marine mammals on Earth pack chains of extraordinary islands, created by powerful volcanic forces far below them. Meanwhile, the nutrient-rich upwellings create profusions of life in some areas, and the abyssal depths host a world of bizarre creatures in others.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: On a visit to Kent, Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen head to Canterbury Cathedral to learn how it helped keep hundreds fed during the Second World War. The pair also call in at a train shed that is now home to a flourishing food market, before popping across to Whitstable to sample its lesser-known delicacy of whelks. At Kent's county cricket ground Mason has a go at leather on willow, before everything stops for tea.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Hungary v Portugal. A chance to see the Euro 2016 Group F encounter at Parc Olympique Lyonnais.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Chef Ainsley Harriott and Strictly Come Dancing judge Len Goodman's road trip takes the pair to south London, where Len has his first taste of Portuguese and Japanese cuisine. Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a mouth-watering Portuguese chicken inspired by the day's adventures.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble revisit dramatic events at Longleat. The wallabies have gone walk about, Jean Johansson meets some poisonous lizards and world class vets come to assist in saving the rhinos from extinction.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.sub-title: Back in Time for the Weekend
programme.desc: The Ashby-Hawkins family enters the 1990s, a decade where new technology starts to dominate their leisure time. Twelve year-old Seth finally gets his hands on a Game Boy and games console, while the arrival of the internet and a mobile phone marks the start of how many spend their spare time now. Steph and Rob try out line-dancing, and fitness star Mr Motivator puts them through their paces. Tommy Walsh and Linda Barker also turn up to help the family give the house and garden a 90s makeover.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan and Ellie Harrison reflect on an unprecedented year and nature's power to help people cope with such challenging times. Wildlife sound recordist Gary Moore searches for a 'lost' dawn chorus on the Knepp Estate in West Sussex, where numbers of rare songbirds like the nightingale are beginning to recover. Gillian Burke explains how she has been helping with a survey of one of Cornwall's much-loved mammals, the grey seal.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don makes space in the writing garden for plants that will flower later in the season and plants out tender vegetables for late summer harvests. Cameras revisit Frances Tophill's allotment to join her as she demonstrates an unusual method of reducing weeds and also catch up with a gardener in Wales who has dedicated years to breeding some very special varieties of rhododendrons. Plus, Joe Swift heads to Devon to find out about the design and planting challenges faced when creating a coastal garden.(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The host enjoys an online conversation with Gregory Porter who, over the past decade, has brought jazz to the masses with his contemporary take, fast becoming one of the best-selling artists of the genre. Last year he launched podcast The Hang, which sees him chat to old and new friends from Jeff Goldblum to Annie Lennox and is planning to release his sixth album, All Rise, in August. Jools and Gregory discuss the artists he loves and is influenced by, illustrated with performances from the Later archive. The programme retains its focus on new music and artists, here spotlighting Dublin band Fontaines DC.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents a comprehensive analysis of the day's events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a selection of memorable moments where members of the panel competed with each other to secure investment in a promising entrepreneur's product. Pitches featured include an umbrella dispensing machine which won the show's first ever investment, a travel toy company that all five panellists wanted a share of, and a commercial submarine business.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to create a deep forest with tall majestic trees, rain puddles and dark fresh air.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: With chainsaws, adzes and more than 30 different chisels, master Maori carver Logan Okiwi Shipgood crafts a beautiful 6ft tall 'pou' statue from native New Zealand timber.(n)

programme.title: Shock and Awe: The Story of Electricity
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili charts how scientists discovered the connection between electricity and magnetism, and explains why this breakthrough gave mankind far greater control over electrical forces. He also reveals that their findings were crucial to the development of electricity as a mass-produced source of power, leading to innovations in communications, manufacturing, transport and technology.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre examines how continued success for the unit could only be achieved with ever more dangerous and ambitious missions, while David Stirling's new second-in-command Captain Paddy Mayne was an officer as unpredictable and dangerous as the phase of war that was about to begin. The historian also reveals how many SAS members were captured by the Germans - and hears the extraordinary story of two of them who outran their pursuers by surviving for three days in the desert without food or fresh water.(n)

programme.title: The Making of Spain
programme.sub-title: Blood and Gold: The Making of Spain with Simon Sebag Montefiore
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore explores the history of Spain, beginning with the country's early years, when Iberia was a minor province of Carthage - then the most coveted of Rome's colonies - through to Spain's Muslim age and the Cordoba Caliphate.(n)

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates this documentary exploring the vocal ranges of marine mammals. Sperm whales produce clicks to locate giant squid two miles beneath the ocean surface, and narwhals use a similar technique to find breathing holes in Arctic ice floes. Bottlenose dolphins communicate with fishermen in Brazil, humpbacks offer haunting operatic performances and boto dolphins create bat-like ultrasonic calls to help find fish in the murky Amazon River.(n)

programme.title: The High Art of the Low Countries
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the countries of Holland and Belgium, famous for their tulips and windmills, but which were actually forged through conflict and division. He examines how a period of economic prosperity, driven by the secular middle class, led to the Dutch Golden Age of the 17th century, during which time the concept of oil painting was conceived, and the master painters such as Rembrandt and Vermeer found success.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Mrs Bennet spots an opportunity to marry off another of her daughters with the arrival of clergyman Mr Collins. However, Elizabeth is unable to take his proposal seriously, being too preoccupied with a handsome newcomer who poisons her mind further against Mr Darcy. Alison Steadman, Jennifer Ehle and David Bamber star in Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to create a deep forest with tall majestic trees, rain puddles and dark fresh air.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: With chainsaws, adzes and more than 30 different chisels, master Maori carver Logan Okiwi Shipgood crafts a beautiful 6ft tall 'pou' statue from native New Zealand timber.(n)

programme.title: Shock and Awe: The Story of Electricity
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili charts how scientists discovered the connection between electricity and magnetism, and explains why this breakthrough gave mankind far greater control over electrical forces. He also reveals that their findings were crucial to the development of electricity as a mass-produced source of power, leading to innovations in communications, manufacturing, transport and technology.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a trail covered in fresh white snow, surrounded by warm colours and fuzzy bark trees, as he paints a brisk quiet day.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: Following Hawaiian artist Dalani Tanahy as she creates a traditional hula skirt from tree bark, symbolising the resurgence of indigenous pride and identity.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Wickham's elopement with Lydia makes Elizabeth see him for what he really is, while Bingley proposes to Jane - an offer she readily accepts. Colin Firth and Jennifer Ehle star.(n)

programme.title: Madame Tussaud: A Legend in Wax
programme.desc: The story of the woman behind the worldwide waxworks empire, revealing how his single mother and entrepreneur travelled across the Channel to England, where she overcame the odds to establish her enduring brand. Determined to leave an account of who she was and the times she lived through, her memoirs, letters and papers offer a unique insight into the creation of the extraordinary empire that bears her name.(n)

programme.title: Chasing the Moon
programme.desc: Part one of two. The documentary about the space race recalls how the success of 1968's Apollo 8 mission led to underlying questions about the space programme from politicised young Americans - but Nasa pushed ahead regardless. Competition escalated among the training astronauts and at the beginning of 1969 the crew for Apollo 11 was announced - Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin and Michael Collins - and in mid-July crowds flooded Cocoa Beach in anticipation of the historic launch.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Eyes Down! The Story of Bingo
programme.desc: In January 1961, the game of bingo turned into a multimillion-pound business almost overnight. This documentary explores how what started out as an occasional seaside flutter became the favoured pastime of nearly a quarter of the population, leading to moral panic about the dangers of easy prize-money. Narrated by Sarah Lancashire.(n)

programme.title: My Astonishing Self: Gabriel Byrne on George Bernard Shaw
programme.desc: The actor explores the life, works and passions of the playwright, examining Shaw's radical and unapologetic political thinking and ability to charm and satirise the establishment.(n)

programme.title: Fabric of Britain
programme.desc: Paul Martin explores the secret history of wallpaper, from lavish 17th-century offerings designed to look like embroidered textiles, through to the interior designs of the modern day. He reveals how 19th-century technological revolutions allowed designers such as Augustus Pugin and William Morris to correct Britain's poor reputation for decorative aesthetics, and explores how the UK's designers fought off the advance of the white-washed facades favoured by the likes of 20th-century modernist innovator Le Corbusier.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a trail covered in fresh white snow, surrounded by warm colours and fuzzy bark trees, as he paints a brisk quiet day.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: Following Hawaiian artist Dalani Tanahy as she creates a traditional hula skirt from tree bark, symbolising the resurgence of indigenous pride and identity.(n)

programme.title: Madame Tussaud: A Legend in Wax
programme.desc: The story of the woman behind the worldwide waxworks empire, revealing how his single mother and entrepreneur travelled across the Channel to England, where she overcame the odds to establish her enduring brand. Determined to leave an account of who she was and the times she lived through, her memoirs, letters and papers offer a unique insight into the creation of the extraordinary empire that bears her name.(n)

programme.title: Benjamin Britten on Camera
programme.desc: Britten was one of the great classical composers of the broadcasting age and his music was regularly performed on radio and TV throughout his working life. This documentary outlines his broadcasting legacy by meeting some of the musicians he wrote for, visiting the Suffolk coast that inspired so much of his work, and hearing from the producers who brought his music to millions. With contributions by David Attenborough, Michael Crawford, Humphrey Burton and soprano April Cantelo. Narrated by James Naughtie.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first shown on October 12, featuring performances by Sinitta, Cliff Richard, Alice Cooper, Deborah Harry, Chris Rea, Richard Marx and Living in a Box.(n)

programme.title: Kermode and Mayo's Home Entertainment Service
programme.desc: With cinemas closed and the nation in lockdown, the UK's most-trusted screen-guides turn their attention from the big screen to the small screen for the first time. Broadcast from Mark and Simon's respective homes, the duo turn their attention to new sci-fi thriller series Snowpiercer, just out on Netflix, while popping in for a chat is actor Rafe Spall, currently appearing in Trying on Apple TV+.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 19 October 1989, featuring Martika, Sybil and Deborah Harry.(n)

programme.title: The Beatles: Made on Merseyside
programme.desc: Few dispute that the Beatles defined 1960s music and popular culture like no other band, but how John, Paul, George and Ringo made the journey from Merseyside teenagers to international pop stars is less known. Director Alan Byron's documentary recounts the rise of the band as American rock 'n' roll and rhythm and blues dragged post-war Liverpool into one of the most vibrant music cities ever with the Mersey Beat sound.(n)

programme.title: Sings the Beatles
programme.desc: A look back through the BBC's archives at memorable covers of classic Beatles songs. Included are a performance of Day Tripper by Sandie Shaw, Shirley Bassey's version of Something, the Carpenters' rendition of Help!, Oasis's reinvention of I Am the Walrus, and Joe Cocker's chart-topping interpretation of With a Little Help from My Friends. Plus, Paul McCartney's solo performance of Lady Madonna.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Another chance to see Steve Wright present a special edition first broadcast in 2000 to mark what would have been John Lennon's 60th year. Featured songs include Imagine, Jealous Guy, Instant Karma, Mind Games and Stand By Me, and the programme includes an interview with Yoko Ono.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the five remaining contestants take part in the quarter final, creating garments and trying their hand at techniques from around the world. Firstly, the sewers must follow a pattern like they have never encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a Filipino Terno Blouse. In the Transformation Challenge, the competitors are asked to turn Provencal tablecloths from France into a brand new outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a dramatic Flamenco skirt from Southern Spain in the final challenge.(n)

programme.desc: Ben watches in disbelief as Susan clears his wardrobe, ditching the old clothes that remind him of his free and easy past. programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee interrupts Lucy's favourite TV programme by plonking himself down next to her on the sofa, eating her chocolates and talking loudly all the way through the dialogue. But when he suddenly can't remember the name of the leading actor and his hypochondria tells him he is losing his memory, Lucy's evening is completely ruined.(n)

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian turns his hand to the intricate business of cake art, soon discovering that the devil is in the fiddly detail. A prestigious competition is on the horizon, offering the opportunity for Rhod's ambitious creation to wow the judges - or make people laugh for all the wrong reasons.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Comedy about two sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who have no idea the other exists until their father dies. Cat wants to spend as much time as possible with Cathy to make up for the last 28 years and, as they were born just five days apart, decide to organise a joint birthday party. In the wake of their dad's demise, the pair struggle to find his will, while their mothers war over which family the late two-timer loved most. Stephen Tompkinson co-stars, alongside Ellie White, Lauren Socha and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: Big Night at the Bingo - Our Lives
programme.desc: A look inside a bingo hall in the heart of West Belfast, meeting some of the regulars who have known each other for many years, through thick and thin. The programme follows bingo members including serenely composed Eilish, who was taught how to play by her grandmother when she was in her teens, and hard-working Margaret, who juggles her bingo alongside work, a college course, school pick-ups and supporting her husband as he deals with health problems.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore more family heirlooms, including a tired and dilapidated a rocking horse that holds huge sentimental significance for its owner and a 1930s pinball machine. Other items are a Victorian device used for measuring weather pressure and a pair of precious plates.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the five remaining contestants take part in the quarter final, creating garments and trying their hand at techniques from around the world. Firstly, the sewers must follow a pattern like they have never encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a Filipino Terno Blouse. In the Transformation Challenge, the competitors are asked to turn Provencal tablecloths from France into a brand new outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a dramatic Flamenco skirt from Southern Spain in the final challenge.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: Comedy in which actors David Tennant and Michael Sheen play themselves as they prepare for a stage production while under lockdown. The Good Omens co-stars were due to put on a play in the West End before the production was put on hold because of the coronavirus pandemic. Not wanting to let his big break pass him by, director Simon Evans must do his best to convince his doubtful actors to carry on rehearsals online.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: With rehearsals due to begin, director Simon goes missing. Michael and David embrace the opportunity to skive off from their duties, but one of them gets caught breaking the law. Georgia is due to deliver her novel to her agent and asks David to stand in and help with the kids.(n)

programme.title: The Mysterious Murder of Nipsey Hussle
programme.desc: Ben Zand sets out to unravel the mystery of rapper and entrepreneur Nipsey Hussle's murder, which took place in March 2019, travelling to Los Angeles to meet those closest to him.(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Journalist Nikki Fox teams up with scientists at Liverpool John Moores University to discover what takeaways do to our health, what's really in them and how people can kick the habit. In a unique experiment, 15 volunteers eat nothing but takeaways for two weeks and undergo a barrage of tests, from blood sugar and body fat to gut bacteria and mental sharpness. The results reveal the shocking truth about how takeaways affect our bodies and brains.(n)

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: Topical debate on the big issues affecting Scotland and beyond. Members of the public have an hour to put their questions to a panel of politicians and other public figures about the biggest stories and the things they care about the most.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: After a career serving all over the world, army veteran Jason, his wife Esther, and their four children have finally put down roots - but the trouble is their Suffolk garden is little more than a concrete car park. The couple have a budget of £3,000 to try to create a space where they can enjoy sitting out as a family. As the military is a big part of this family's life, they want to see the resulting garden incorporate some remembrance poppies. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Stowmarket to see how they can help Jason and Esther to realise their dreams.(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a fashion makeover show, with five stylists advising a diverse range of people in need of a significant style overhaul. In the first edition they help Chris, a 6ft 5in personal hygiene salesman who only wears black, and 5ft 3in Manny, who has been wearing children's clothes for years and is looking for a grown-up style with sass. PE teacher Rachel wants to swap gym wear for glamour, while warehouse employee Karen is worried that her work wardrobe is creeping into her weekend.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: An urgent call is made by a man whose wife is in cardiac arrest, with a doctor and paramedic team from North West Air Ambulance immediately dispatched to assist on the job. Back in control, a call is in progress for a patient suffering chest pains as a result of cocaine binge. Meanwhile out on the road, crewmates Chris and V are dispatched to a category-three call for a patient who's fallen. However, once the patient is safely treated the couple's dogs become the focus of the paramedics' attention. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is house hunting with a couple who have a budget of £650,000 and want to quit the city to find a house with great entertaining potential in the Cheshire countryside. Sonali also delves into the region's manufacturing heritage at one of the country's best preserved textile mills from the Industrial Revolution.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an unusual musical instrument, a wartime phone being sold for charity, and a cake stand with cakes thrown in. Presented by Nigel Havers.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and James Braxton's penultimate leg takes them to Lowestoft and Cromer. Along the way, Izzie is charmed by a snake and an art deco necklace, while James goes crabbing and gets all fired up by a cannonball. But who will come out on top at auction in Banbury?(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a fashion makeover show, with five stylists advising a diverse range of people in need of a significant style overhaul. In the first edition they help Chris, a 6ft 5in personal hygiene salesman who only wears black, and 5ft 3in Manny, who has been wearing children's clothes for years and is looking for a grown-up style with sass. PE teacher Rachel wants to swap gym wear for glamour, while warehouse employee Karen is worried that her work wardrobe is creeping into her weekend.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: An urgent call is made by a man whose wife is in cardiac arrest, with a doctor and paramedic team from North West Air Ambulance immediately dispatched to assist on the job. Back in control, a call is in progress for a patient suffering chest pains as a result of cocaine binge. Meanwhile out on the road, crewmates Chris and V are dispatched to a category-three call for a patient who's fallen. However, once the patient is safely treated the couple's dogs become the focus of the paramedics' attention. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston.(n)

Nigel then invites the dealers to bid against one another to secure a vintage fairground game, a 19th-century Chinese inkwell, a 1950s toy pedal car and a Victorian watchman's clock.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Everton winger Theo Walcott, 2006 Commonwealth heptathlon champion Kelly Sotherton, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin and Welsh rugby great Scott Quinnell join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz.(n)

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country at Home
programme.desc: Leading country music star Nathan Carter invites cameras into his new home for a performance of some of his most popular songs, as well as a duet with brother Jake.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee interrupts Lucy's favourite TV programme by plonking himself down next to her on the sofa, eating her chocolates and talking loudly all the way through the dialogue. But when he suddenly can't remember the name of the leading actor and his hypochondria tells him he is losing his memory, Lucy's evening is completely ruined.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Comedy about two sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who have no idea the other exists until their father dies. Cat wants to spend as much time as possible with Cathy to make up for the last 28 years and, as they were born just five days apart, decide to organise a joint birthday party. In the wake of their dad's demise, the pair struggle to find his will, while their mothers war over which family the late two-timer loved most. Stephen Tompkinson co-stars, alongside Ellie White, Lauren Socha and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: It's John's grandmother's birthday, but he has completely forgotten to post her card. Will he be able to put things right on the journey to work? Comedy about the daily commute of supermarket employees John Redmond and Kayleigh Kitson, who have been thrown together in a company car share scheme, with each trip bringing fresh insight into their lives, as well as twists and turns in their unlikely relationship. Co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson, with Danny Swarsbrick.(n)

The host also relives the moment Gary Barlow surprises the audience by singing along to their karaoke versions of some classic Take That songs.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Scandal at the Post Office - Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of sub-postmasters were jailed or financially ruined after computers said money was missing from their branches, but the Post Office has admitted that its Horizon system can make mistakes. But when did senior managers find this out, and did they continue to prosecute postmasters for stealing when they knew technology could be to blame? Reporter Nick Wallis investigates what could be Britain's biggest-ever miscarriage of justice scandal and uncovers evidence of a cover-up at the Post Office.(n)

programme.title: Lon Le Linda
programme.desc: Linda Macleod and her friends prepare a number of tasty dishes. In this episode, Linda is joined by Fionnlagh and Elizabeth to make a multi-coloured salad with tuna, pasta and various vegetables.(n)

programme.title: Na Floogals
programme.desc: When the Floogals scatter a jigsaw, they have difficulty putting the puzzle back together.(n)

programme.title: Baile Mhuilinn
programme.desc: Gaelic songs and stories for children learning to read, featuring the letter S.(n)

programme.desc: Julie Fowlis reads An Tìgeir a Thàinig gu Teatha, written and illustrated by Judith Kerr and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart.(n)

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall
programme.desc: Short drama Mystique No 375, set in Largs, south-west Scotland. Ten-year-old Kriselle lives with her father, who manages a rundown seaside theatre. Kriselle is wise beyond her years, and has a hankering for being on stage herself - but the theatre is fast losing money and may have to close down.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments that can be tried at home, including how to create a rainbow in a jar and build a mini-beach in a backyard.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Documentary about an adventurous young grey wolf living in the wilds of northern Canada. Winona must learn to get along with her pack, and bring down prey more than twice her size.(n)

programme.title: Sugh an Eorna (History of Whisky)
programme.desc: John Carmichael presents a six-part series celebrating whisky through the centuries, beginning with a visit to the distilleries in the Borders and the cities of the Lowlands, and a sample of the region's best-known malts.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming in remote and unspoilt lochs and rivers, visiting some of his favourite swimming locations throughout Scotland.(n)

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: A chat with Màrtainn arouses suspicion in Màiri, and she is forced to return to the city to find out the truth for herself. A stranger arrives on the island who is sure to set the cat amongst the pigeons.(n)

programme.title: Port

programme.title: DIY le Donnie
programme.desc: Donnie MacLeod is in Barra supporting Cobhair Bharraigh, a charitable organisation providing day care to residents in Barra and Vatersay. The sitting room walls have been damaged where the chairs have been scraping, a new toilet seat is required and one of the kitchen taps needs repairing. The DIY expert also reveals a new shower room he has completed in his own home.(n)

programme.desc: Granny shows Calum how to make patterns using objects including cubes, cylinders, spheres and cones.(n)

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald reads Am Madadh-allaidh mu Dheireadh, written and illustrated by Mini Grey and translated into Gaelic by Donald John Macleod.(n)

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Penfold and Danger Mouse arrive at the UN to scenes of quarrelling diplomats, caused by a sabotaged translation system, and also discover Ian has become a fan of Crumhorn.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments that can be tried at home, including how to grow coral and pass a football through a postcard. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.sub-title: Momo am Macaque Iapanach (Momo the Japanese Macaque)
programme.desc: Documentary about a Japanese macaque called Momo. The programme follows the monkey through the four seasons of his first year as he learns all the skills and tricks he needs to survive.(n)

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Lewis crofter Sweeny gears up for egg production, and has a school visiit. However, crofting's many challenges are all too apparent when the first major winter storm hits - and there's a death to contend with.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Short films highlighting the importance of select cultural phenomena, including a women's football match between England and France, and footage of nuns at work.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: An Taigh-Fuine/The Bakery
programme.desc: Allan and Lewis prepare to cater for the EDF festival, one of Benbecula's biggest events, while the Macleans are also making preparations for a wedding in Balivanaich Hall.(n)

programme.title: Bliadhna na Sgoile (My School Year)
programme.desc: Following a school year through the eyes of a teacher, a parent and pupils at the Gaelic unit at Gilcomstoun Primary School in Aberdeen. The bond between pupil and teacher is strong and there are plenty of tears and laughter on this emotional journey. Narrated by Patricia Macleod.(n)

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Karen Matheson performs versions of Calum Sgaire and Cànan nan Gàidheal, with an international cast of musicians, and with Galician musicians Xabier Diaz and Guadi Galego.(n)

programme.title: Ameireaga air Film (America in Colour)
programme.desc: Life in early 1900s America, shown in colour for the first time.(n)

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: A performance by innovative band Heisk at Na Trads in 2019.(n)

programme.title: Belladrum
programme.desc: The Supernaturals sing I Don't Think So at Belladrum '18.(n)

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Uisdean and Roddy cook with alcohol, preparing beercan chicken, mojito cheesecake and hake in a spicy batter, accompanied by tartar sauce and a hot salad.(n)

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service.(n)

programme.title: Alba Today

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: The duo investigate heavy and light.(n)

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: Pup and Kit examine inside and outside.(n)

programme.title: Gudrun - A' Bhana-phrionnsa Lochlannach
programme.desc: Gudrun and Frida get inspiration for their May Day festival dance when King Sigurd takes them into the woods to see capercaillies.(n)

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte

programme.desc: The long-serving mailman faces a long and rocky path when attempting to deliver a hefty parcel.(n)

programme.title: BB agus Bellag
programme.desc: Two baby birds become lost, so Bellag helps them find their way home.(n)

programme.title: Blàrag a' Bhò (Connie the Cow)
programme.sub-title: Spors san t-Side Fhuar (A Cold Weather Adventure)
programme.desc: Connie has an adventure in cold weather.(n)

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Dolina MacLennan reads Goldilocks agus Dìreach Aon Mhathan, written and illustrated by Leigh Hodgkinson and translated into Gaelic by Donald J MacLeod.(n)

programme.title: Sràid nan Sgread (Scream Street)
programme.desc: Luke comes home to an unexpected guest as Otto moves in with the Watsons.(n)

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Eòseph has cerebral palsy, but is determined to walk to his own birthday party.(n)

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Things slip out of control when Peter's artistic creations take on minds of their own.(n)

programme.title: Prosbaig
programme.desc: Donald Morrison recalls a campaign for a hospice to be built in the Highlands, the plight of homeless Gaels in Glasgow and the story behind a shell-covered bus based in South Uist.(n)

programme.title: Orain Oir
programme.desc: Delving into the Gaelic music archive to pick out the best performances through the decades.(n)

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Gregor Macleod is in Benbecula to taste crisp puff pastry pasties filled with the freshest Uist crab meat and melt-in-the-mouth leeks. In Beauly he meets a woman who uses amaretti biscuits paired with raspberries to add texture and flavour to a pudding.(n)

programme.title: Spors
programme.desc: Calum Macaulay and Derek Mackay provide an insight into St Johnstone FC, getting to the heart of the key stories on and off the pitch.(n)

programme.title: Spors

programme.title: An Bronntanas
programme.desc: A man returns to Ireland to discover his family factory is facing bankruptcy, and during a lifeboat rescue mission he and the team find a murdered woman on a boat. They attempt to retrieve the body, but find something else on board that will change their lives for ever. Thriller following a Connemara lifeboat crew, starring Dara Devaney.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally is swayed by Yasmeen's change of heart when she enters a not guilty plea, while back on the street, Geoff is furious and tells Tim they need to show a united front. Gary tracks down Laura to the address in Rochdale and promises to ensure Rick keeps paying her living expenses if she'll take Kelly back under her wing.(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: A special series looking back at Bradley and Barney's previous American adventures. The first episode sees the pair start in Los Angeles. Kicking things off in Venice beach, the pair get in shape with a game of volleyball and pump some weights at Muscle Beach, but the following the day Bradley is pushed to his limits when Barney reveals he has arranged for them to go skydiving. Leaving Los Angeles behind them, they head into the desert towards Arizona as they venture onto the famous Route 66, where the pair head off to Cowboy College.(n)

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Sheron and Billy Dawson check in for some pool-side fun, bringing along their teenage son Rob, young daughter Jodie, and Grandad Eddie, to claim the sun-loungers left vacant following the Garveys' departure from the vacation spot. Meanwhile, resort boss Joyce Temple-Savage is desperate for new staff, and must choose between efficient, goody two-shoes Amber and the outrageously flirtatious Marcus, and Troy returns to Benidorm with Jacqueline and Kenneth to scatter Donald's ashes in the place he loved best. Julie Graham, Steve Edge and Bobby Knutt join the cast of the comedy drama set in the sun-kissed Solana resort.(n)

programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. This time around, a delivery drone drops a painful parcel, a quad-bike rolls into a lake, and a baby steals a smartphone and spends a fortune on concert tickets.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part special to begin the second series, the actor and film-maker examines the real-life impact of dementia. He meets Scott Mitchell, the husband of his former on-screen mum Barbara Windsor, who was diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease in 2014. Ross also travels to Leatherhead, Surrey, where he stays with Jerry Beckman, an actor, magician and keen sportsman who has early-onset dementia and Parkinson's disease.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: In a reality TV special, former Gogglebox star and 2016 Queen of the Jungle Scarlett Moffatt takes on 2019 Love Island contestant Curtis Pritchard. The pair - along with their families - go head to head in the knockout tournament, hoping to advance to the next round. Presented by Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: As life in lockdown continues, Martin Lewis has up-to-the-minute advice for these financially uncertain times. He'll be taking viewers' questions on how the ongoing crisis affects family finances, jobs and consumer rights, and offers money-saving tips and tricks.(n)

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the dramatic story of an amateur pilot who built his own plane, but then lost his propeller mid-flight and had to try to land it without any power. A local resident in northern California tries to flee what goes down in history as the state's deadliest wildfire, and a cliff collapse at a popular tourist spot in Greece causes chaos.(n)

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.sub-title: Britain's Best Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The expert walker visits the Isle of Man, where her favourite island walk has it all - an ancient Manx river glen, the world's biggest mining waterwheel and a climb that culminates with spectacular views of England, Scotland and Ireland. With steam trains and electric trams on the TT island famous for Thomas the Tank Engine, her latest trek promises to be action-packed.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla is cornered by a mystery girl in the Rovers and Tim starts to wonder about Geoff's protestations of innocence after Sally tells him she wants Geoff out of the house. Ken fears that Daniel is neglecting himself and Toyah worries about Leanne after she tells her that Oliver has regressed.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey is very welcoming when a surprise visitor turns up in the form of the attractive Emma, although Sharon is suspicious of the newcomer and Dorien is shocked to the core. Travis, on the other hand, is over the moon. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Charlie Quirke.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is called in when David Kenworthy, the son of a prominent local family, is found murdered on the beach at Whitley Bay. A witness spotted arcade owner Larry Crowe arguing with David on the night of the killing, and a search of the suspect's property uncovers a bloodied baseball bat. However, Larry has a cast-iron alibi and it's clear he wasn't the only person the victim met that night. A second death then leaves Vera facing mounting pressure to crack the case, and struggling to answer the question of why David was drawn to the seafront on the evening of his father's retirement. Brenda Blethyn stars in the murder mystery, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe.(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.sub-title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories: Martin Kemp
programme.desc: The actor and musician joins the host to look back over his life and career. Martin, who found fame as the bassist in Spandau Ballet, before going on to star in EastEnders and The Krays, tells of the brain tumours that nearly killed him and reveals that George Michael helped him find a wife, as well as land his TV soap role. He also opens up about the deaths of his parents within days of each other, and how this prompted his band to get back together.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is called in when David Kenworthy, the son of a prominent local family, is found murdered on the beach at Whitley Bay. A witness spotted arcade owner Larry Crowe arguing with David on the night of the killing, and a search of the suspect's property uncovers a bloodied baseball bat. However, Larry has a cast-iron alibi and it's clear he wasn't the only person the victim met that night. A second death then leaves Vera facing mounting pressure to crack the case, and struggling to answer the question of why David was drawn to the seafront on the evening of his father's retirement. Brenda Blethyn stars in the murder mystery, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe.(n)

programme.desc: Jeffrey and George get into the festive spirit by challenging each other to a game of computer tennis. Festive instalment of the comedy, from 1978, starring Brian Murphy and Norman Eshley.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper is astonished and thrilled when George buys her an expensive present for their anniversary. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Owing to a little white lie on his income tax returns, Mr Roper must quickly acquire a son. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress has his work cut out as a series of crimes linked to an old farming feud turns deadly. But when the evidence fails to add up it is up to the bobbies to prove who is responsible before someone gets killed. Meanwhile, Vernon is inspired to screen films in the village hall. Rural police drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes, William Simons, Tricia Penrose and Jason Durr.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave apologises to Kathy for how he acted. Zoe visits Frank but leaves before she has to face Kim. Ned tells Eric that he won't be getting any more money out of the Glovers.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak, Butch and Sam give Mandy a peace offering. Kim talks Frank into giving Dave some money to start afresh somewhere else. Mandy's Munch Box opens and awaits customers.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim celebrates his 40th birthday, but feels he hasn't achieved anything in life. Reg learns the police have been asking questions about him at Firmans.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz tells Jim she's sick of his aggressive nature. Eric offers Reg a promotion. Vicky is amused when Steve is jealous of her doing business with an old friend.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A fox-hunt turns sour when a tramp is found dead in the woods, and matters are further complicated by the murder of the chief suspect's father. As DCI Barnaby investigates, he concludes it is only a matter of time before another member of the tight-knit community suffers the same fate. Guest starring James Bolam.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress has his work cut out as a series of crimes linked to an old farming feud turns deadly. But when the evidence fails to add up it is up to the bobbies to prove who is responsible before someone gets killed. Meanwhile, Vernon is inspired to screen films in the village hall. Rural police drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes, William Simons, Tricia Penrose and Jason Durr.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Edith's big day finally arrives, but will her enthusiasm convince the family that Strallan's age doesn't matter? Meanwhile, Mrs Hughes anxiously awaits her results, Anna makes a breakthrough to help Bates, and Mary takes matters into her own hands to secure the estate's future - but the personal cost could be high. Costume drama, starring Laura Carmichael, Robert Bathurst, Phyllis Logan, Joanne Froggatt and Michelle Dockery.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barrister is killed with a lethal dose of morphine and suspicion falls on ex-convict Grady Felton, who has recently returned to the village where he committed murder years earlier. However, he has a strong alibi and Barnaby thinks he must be working with an accomplice - until Grady himself is targeted in an arson attack. Detective drama, guest starring Sharon Duce, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A local woman is killed when an experiment in biological warfare goes tragically wrong. Sam also falls victim to a potentially fatal illness as a result of the project. Foyle is forced to head into the most secretive and dangerous areas of wartime research, unearthing secrets that cannot be revealed even to the prime minister. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Another chance to see the feature-length first episode of the popular detective drama. Inspector Jack Frost's investigation into the case of a missing child uncovers a skeleton chained to a locked strongbox that tantalisingly turns out to be empty, but provides evidence of a crime committed 30 years previously. David Jason stars as the irascible detective, with Bruce Alexander, Matt Bardock and Claire Hackett.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper goes into hospital with suspected appendicitis and George looks forward to some peace and quiet - but Jerry shows up on his doorstep with other ideas. Roy Kinnear guest stars.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George has already denounced the neighbours as toffee-nosed snobs, and events make Mildred wonder whether he could be right. Queenie Watts and Michael Robbins guest star, with Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin and Jo line up to star in Chrissy's home video, but Mr Roper is interested in a different kind of film. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A local woman is killed when an experiment in biological warfare goes tragically wrong. Sam also falls victim to a potentially fatal illness as a result of the project. Foyle is forced to head into the most secretive and dangerous areas of wartime research, unearthing secrets that cannot be revealed even to the prime minister. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor is suspected of involvement in the theft of a priceless Ming vase. Mike tries to discover the reason for Merton's visit to headquarters and Gina starts acting like Aidensfield's answer to Mata Hari. Nostalgic drama, guest starring Leslie Grantham.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris attempts to find out why Kim is back at Home Farm. Susie plans a charity night for the Glovers. Zak and Butch steal a freezer, but there's a shock waiting for them inside it.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It's Roy's birthday and he announces that he's left school. Frank is determined that Kim will never be allowed to forget what she has done to him.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg tells Eric that he won't be taking the job for personal reasons. Liz is upset by the way she and Jim keep annoying each other. Kevin gets a new assistant at the garage.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des is disturbed by his feelings for Liz. Kevin is infuriated by Barry and his passion for punctuality. Jim cooks Liz a special meal to show her he loves her.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits his Aunt Alice, who is recovering from an operation at a nursing home, and finds himself in the middle of a mystery. A resident has been found dead and the official verdict is a heart attack, but as rumours of fraud and deception abound, Alice's friend believes someone may have conspired to commit murder.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor is suspected of involvement in the theft of a priceless Ming vase. Mike tries to discover the reason for Merton's visit to headquarters and Gina starts acting like Aidensfield's answer to Mata Hari. Nostalgic drama, guest starring Leslie Grantham.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Branson's political views land him in trouble and Sybil's loyalty is tested to the limit, while Ethel makes a difficult decision about her son's future. Anna wonders what is causing Bates' silence, and Carson begins recruiting a footman, with promising candidate Jimmy (Ed Speleers, Eragon) sparking immediate interest below stairs. Costume drama, starring Allen Leech, Jessica Brown Findlay, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt, Brendan Coyle and Jim Carter.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The irascible medic and Louisa embark on what seems to be a perfect first date at a classical music performance in the grounds of a country house, but an inappropriate remark from the doctor kills off any chance of romance. Elsewhere, Al worries that Pauline's gambling is getting out of hand when he finds out that her moped has been repossessed. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: News of Martin and Louisa's engagement spreads rapidly through the village, and Aunt Joan is annoyed to hear about it from Dave rather than her nephew. Al steps in to help as Bert struggles to keep the restaurant afloat, while the doctor is puzzled when he learns Beth's knee replacement wound is failing to respond to the medication he prescribed. Comedy drama series, starring Martin Clunes.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is adamant that her hotpot will be as good as Betty's. Raquel is pleased when Curly tells her Leo has been sacked. Vicky discovers Steve has hired a cleaner for their flat.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack plays the breweries off each other to get lower beer prices. Steve accuses Vicky of trying to control him. Some dodgy pies at the Rovers give the pub's customers stomachaches.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emma packs up her stuff. Zak gets a shock when he receives his phone bill. Terry feels relaxed before the big rugby match, confident that Martin Offiah will save the day.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe rushes to the hospital when she hears about Susie's accident. Terry wallows in self-pity after the rugby match defeat. Tina catches Chris going through Frank's personal files.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly patient's sudden death prompts a series of revelations about his decidedly shady past - and the raid he was planning on Ashfordly Bank. Meanwhile, Craddock is offered the chance of promotion, and Vernon embarks on yet another venture - Scripps' Roaming Holidays. Nostalgic drama, starring Philip Franks, Jason Durr and Geoffrey Hughes.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers babysit for their neighbours, but George misses his usual routine - so he nips out to a darts match with the infant in tow. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Eagle-eyed George sees an investment opportunity when Mildred suggests their dog should have puppies. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy's mother makes plans for her daughter's future happiness - and she knows just the man to help. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New sergeant Dennis Merton arrives at Ashfordly police station and soon finds himself embroiled in a CID stakeout - but Ventress's shady past threatens the entire operation. Meanwhile, all efforts to pummel David into the peak of physical fitness look set to come to nothing, until a chance encounter knocks Vernon for six. Nostalgic drama, starring William Simons and Duncan Bell.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim leaves hospital and returns to Frank. Dave tries desperately to speak to Kim. Zak wants Mandy to rename her business to Zak's Snack Shack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff lays into Kim for destroying Dave. Terry gets ready for his lunch date with Elaine. Dave asks Kathy to go and see Kim for him and find out why she's back with Frank.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy worries that Deirdre might be on the verge of a breakdown. Jack relishes being pampered by the brewery. Vera lays on a buffet for Daniel's christening tea.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin returns from holiday to find the garage shut and Jim sacked. Mike urges Deirdre to pull herself together. Reg is asked by the police to take part in an identity parade.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New sergeant Dennis Merton arrives at Ashfordly police station and soon finds himself embroiled in a CID stakeout - but Ventress's shady past threatens the entire operation. Meanwhile, all efforts to pummel David into the peak of physical fitness look set to come to nothing, until a chance encounter knocks Vernon for six. Nostalgic drama, starring William Simons and Duncan Bell.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: With the estate's future hanging in the balance, Mary hopes to receive a bailout from her grandmother Martha - and with Violet's help, tries to make sure Downton puts on a bigger show than ever before with a grand party for friends and neighbours. Meanwhile, Mrs Hughes hears worrying news, and O'Brien's efforts to boost Alfred's progress meets with disapproval from Thomas. Costume drama, starring Michelle Dockery, Shirley MacLaine and Maggie Smith.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oswald Cooper is the prime suspect in the rape of a teenage girl, but presents an alibi supported by three pillars of the community. When Cooper is murdered, Lewis discovers a web of deceit going back 20 years, in which the dead man was used by the trio to cover up a sordid series of secrets - but the case soon takes on a personal dimension for the inspector. Kwame Kwei-Armah, Sean McGinley, Richard McCabe and Daniela Nardini guest star, with Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Back from sick leave, Morse uncovers new evidence concerning the year-long investigation into the murder of Yvonne Harrison, but has to cope with playing second fiddle to long-standing sidekick Lewis - and there are questions about whether he is fit enough to handle the case. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Clapham Cook
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth.(n)
